meta.title: Lé Vibe Cline-first migration execution manifest
meta.workspace_summary: Primary product focus is now Cline-first agent experience in the Lé Vibe IDE. Engineering priority is to remove Continue-specific runtime/installer/docs logic and replace with Cline + Ollama + model bootstrap so fresh installs launch agent-ready without manual repair.
meta.rag_notes: Authority remains docs/PRODUCT_SPEC.md where conflicts exist. .lvibe guides execution order and operating constraints for this migration.
meta.cline_transition_note: Strict priority: finish all tasks in epic-cline-e2e-migration before any non-migration cleanup. If blocked twice without new evidence, escalate with a concrete unblock task or decision request.
meta.ai_pilot_note: Local-first policy: Ollama remains primary local runtime; Cline must default to local provider and declared fallback model policy.

session_steps.title: Confirm migration objective
session_steps.description: Replace Continue with Cline end-to-end for install, first-run, docs, and agent readiness.

session_steps.title: Scan migration surface
session_steps.description: Inventory installer, launcher, extension, docs, and tests that currently reference Continue.

session_steps.title: Load engineering roles and constraints
session_steps.description: Use this manifest and .lvibe product track to route tasks to senior engineering agents.

session_steps.title: Execute epics in strict order
session_steps.description: Run product.epics sequentially; complete migration epic before other repo cleanup work.

engineer_completion_protocol: Whenever an engineer agent changes code, it must finish with clean, scoped git commits.
Before task completion, engineer agents must push their branch/commits to the remote and report push status.
If push is blocked (permissions/network/policy), mark task blocked with explicit reason and required operator action.

milestones.objective: Fresh install delivers Ollama runtime, selected default model, Cline extension/config, and launches Lé Vibe with agent ready for immediate use.
acceptance: Installer provisions or reuses Ollama and verifies reachability.
Installer/first-run ensures required Cline extension is installed and activated in target editor binary.
Selected default model policy is validated (present or pulled with explicit status and remediation).
User can run lvibe and enter an agent-ready session without manual Continue-era repair scripts.
exit_tests: targeted pytest contracts for Cline install/setup/runtime checks

epics.title: Continue rip-and-replace to Cline with Ollama model bootstrap
epics.description: Primary migration epic. Remove Continue-centric install and runtime assumptions, replace with Cline-first flow across installer, launcher, docs, and tests.

tasks.title: Capture SME feasibility and migration guardrails for Cline on Codium/Open VSX
tasks.notes: Captured in .lvibe/audit/task-c0-cline-sme-feasibility-2026-04-15.md. Highlights: likely feasible on Codium, validate extension distribution path (Open VSX or VSIX fallback), enforce Ollama/model health checks, staged migration with rollback window.
tasks.acceptance_criteria: SME recommendations are recorded and referenced by implementation tasks.

tasks.title: Strip Continue-specific installer/setup hooks and replace with Cline install flow
tasks.notes: Active installer/runtime path now uses install-cline-extension.sh with deterministic retries and explicit remediation. install-le-vibe-local.sh installs Cline by default; launcher auto-setup hook switched to le-vibe-setup-cline; Debian install surface switched from setup-continue artifacts to setup-cline artifacts.
tasks.acceptance_criteria: No active installer path depends on Continue scripts; Cline install path is default and verified.

tasks.title: Implement first-run checks for Ollama, selected model availability, and Cline activation
tasks.notes: Added first-run readiness gate after bootstrap/autosetup: verifies managed Ollama API reachability, selected model presence on managed API, and active Cline extension in target editor. Default lvibe launch now exits with explicit remediation when readiness fails; subcommands continue with structured log evidence.
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Fresh run reaches agent-ready state or emits explicit remediation steps with no silent failure state.

tasks.title: Enforce Cline startup policy to read .lvibe manifest/instructions before edits
tasks.notes: Added shipped Cline bootstrap rule template at .clinerules/00-le-vibe-bootstrap.md and wired default workspace provisioning to seed it idempotently. Template explicitly requires reading .lvibe/session-manifest.json and relevant .lvibe markdown before edits.
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Documented and testable bootstrap rule exists and is shipped with product defaults.

tasks.title: Replace Continue references in docs with Cline-specific operational guidance
tasks.notes: Updated core docs to make Cline the default operational flow: user/maintainer README guidance, Debian README/manpages (including setup command rename docs), and PM stage-map H4 references now point to Cline install/pin behavior.
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Core docs no longer prescribe Continue setup paths; Cline flow is authoritative and internally consistent.

tasks.title: Replace Continue contract coverage with Cline contract/smoke coverage
tasks.notes: Migrated Continue-era contract assertions to Cline behavior for setup/manpage/stage-map/doc contracts and validated startup-readiness contracts. Regression evidence: targeted pytest suite covers Cline installer contracts (`install-cline-extension.sh`, `le-vibe-setup-cline`), first-run readiness (`evaluate_first_run_agent_readiness`), launcher preamble readiness exit behavior, and updated docs/manpage references.
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Regression suite validates Cline install and startup behavior; old Continue-only checks are removed or migrated.

tasks.title: Run full install/smoke closeout and update manifest/doc handoff notes
tasks.notes: Closeout evidence recorded in .lvibe/audit/task-c6-release-readiness-closeout-2026-04-15.md and .lvibe/audit/task-c6-continue-runtime-inventory-2026-04-15.md. Continue blocker remediation is now enforced (install-cline-extension removes continue.continue + stale profile dirs; first-run readiness fails with explicit uninstall remediation if Continue is active), packaged editor extension state is now Cline+YAML only, and lvibe --force-first-run . completes with managed Ollama/model readiness. Required closeout commands were re-run and confirmed the same state: no Continue activation markers in runtime logs, smoke verify-only passes, and full --install remains blocked in this automation context by interactive sudo password requirement during apt install; operator must rerun the same command in an interactive sudo-capable terminal to finalize fresh-install acceptance. Follow-up startup reliability patch and onboarding evidence: .lvibe/audit/task-c6-startup-reliability-followup-2026-04-15.md.
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Evidence exists that fresh install launches Cline-ready Lé Vibe with Ollama/model checks passing.

epics.title: IDE-first repository cleanup (deferred until Cline migration done)
epics.description: Secondary epic retained for later. Do not start while any task-c* in epic-cline-e2e-migration is pending unless operator explicitly overrides.

tasks.title: Re-open IDE-first layout decisions after Cline migration
tasks.notes: Use .lvibe/IDE_FIRST_REPO_CLEANUP_BACKLOG.md after migration milestone is complete.
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Cleanup planning resumes only after migration acceptance.
